Test Summary: Verify Machine Email Detected column is present on Alerts table and shows Yes or No when feature flag is enabled
Steps:
Automation Steps:
1. Enable feature flag: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with payload {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": true}}, header x-netskope-tenantid: <TENANT_ID>
2. Open browser and navigate to /ns#/alerts
3. Assert column header "MACHINE EMAIL DETECTED" is visible in the Alerts table without any column customization
4. For rows with SMTP Proxy access method, assert column cell value is "Yes" or "No"
5. Teardown: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with payload {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": false}}
Expected Result:
- Column header "MACHINE EMAIL DETECTED" is visible in the Alerts table by default
- SMTP Proxy rows show "Yes" or "No" in the column
- Column is positioned to the right of the existing default columns
Label: ai_generated

Test Summary: Verify previously-recorded side panel values are still visible when feature flag is disabled
Steps:
Automation Steps:
1. Enable feature flag: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": true}}, header x-netskope-tenantid: <TENANT_ID>
2. Send a machine-generated email through SMTP proxy at <SMTP_PROXY_VIP>:25 to generate an event with Machine Email Detected = Yes
3. Wait 30s for event to appear in Application Events or Alerts; note the Transaction ID
4. Disable feature flag: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": false}}
5. Reload the Alerts or Application Events page
6. Locate the previously-recorded event by Transaction ID and click its row expand icon
7. Assert "Machine Email Detected: Yes" field is still visible in the side panel
8. Teardown: none
Expected Result:
- Side panel still shows "Machine Email Detected: Yes" for the previously-recorded event after flag is disabled
- "Machine Email Confidence:" is also still shown if it was present when originally recorded
- Table column is absent but side panel data is intact
Label: ai_generated

column15: Verify Export button is present and enabled on Alerts page when Machine Email Detected events exist
column16:
Automation Steps:
1. Enable feature flag: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with payload {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": true}}, header x-netskope-tenantid: <TENANT_ID>
2. Send a machine-generated email through SMTP proxy at <SMTP_PROXY_VIP>:25 to generate at least one alert
3. Navigate to /ns#/alerts and wait for the table to load
4. Assert at least one row is present in the table
5. Locate the Export button: WebDriverWait(driver, 30).until(EC.element_to_be_clickable((By.XPATH, "//button[normalize-space()='Export']")))
6. Assert the Export button is displayed and enabled (not disabled or hidden)
7. Teardown: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with payload {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": false}}
column17:
- Export button is visible and enabled on the Alerts page when rows are present
- Button is clickable (not greyed out or disabled)
- No error is thrown on page when export button is located

Test Summary: Verify Machine Email Detected and Confidence fields display correctly for email with non-ASCII characters in subject and body
Steps:
Automation Steps:
1. Enable feature flag: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with payload {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": true}}, header x-netskope-tenantid: <TENANT_ID>
2. Build email with non-ASCII subject and body using EmailBuilderSmtp:
   builder = EmailBuilderSmtp(smtp_server=<SMTP_PROXY_VIP>, smtp_port=25)
   builder.send_email(from_email="user0@emailskope.com", to_email="fireeyesales2@gmail.com",
       subject=u"Test メール éàü مرحبا",
       body=u"Body with non-ASCII: 中文 éàü العربية")
3. Wait 30s for the event to appear in Alerts or Application Events
4. Navigate to /ns#/alerts (or /ns#/skopeIT) and locate the event row
5. Open the side panel by clicking the row expand icon
6. In the APPLICATION section under "Other Category:", assert "Machine Email Detected:" field is present with value Yes or No
7. If Detected = Yes, assert "Machine Email Confidence:" is present with value Low, Medium, or High
8. Assert that the subject line displayed in the side panel is not garbled (renders correctly or is URL-encoded consistently)
9. Teardown: POST /internal/v1/featureconfigs with payload {"machine-traffic-detection": {"enabled": false}}
Expected Result:
- Email is accepted by the SMTP proxy (no 5xx error during send)
- "Machine Email Detected:" field is present in the side panel with a valid value (Yes or No)
- If Detected = Yes, "Machine Email Confidence:" is present with value Low, Medium, or High
- Field values are not garbled, empty, or showing encoding errors
- Non-ASCII characters in subject do not cause the Detected or Confidence fields to be missing or malformed
Label: ai_generated
